meta.name: JVM Daily
meta.description: Hierarchical taxonomy for JVM ecosystem articles. Classifies by technology area and optional sub-area.

classification_hints.summary: Articles about the Java programming language, JVM runtime internals, JDK releases, and OpenJDK projects. Covers the platform itself — not frameworks or libraries built on it.
topics: Java language features and syntax changes (records, sealed classes, switch expressions, text blocks)
JVM runtime internals (JIT compilation, class loading, bytecode, module system, JFR, JMX)
JDK release announcements, upgrade guides, and deprecation notices
OpenJDK governance, development process, and contributor discussions
JDK performance characteristics (startup, throughput, memory footprint)
not_this_node: Kotlin language features (even if running on JVM) → kotlin
Spring, Quarkus, Micronaut framework usage → spring / quarkus / micronaut
GraalVM native image compilation → graalvm
Build tool features (Gradle, Maven) → build-tooling
Hibernate or database access → data-persistence

classification_hints.summary: Virtual threads, structured concurrency, scoped values — deliverables of Project Loom.
signals: Discusses virtual threads, structured concurrency, or scoped values
References Project Loom, JEP 444, JEP 453, JEP 462, JEP 480, JEP 481, JEP 487
Compares virtual threads with platform threads or reactive programming (Project Reactor, RxJava)
Discusses pinning, carrier threads, or virtual thread scheduling
classification_hints.boundary: If the article is about concurrency in general (thread pools, executors, locks) without specifically discussing virtual threads or Loom, classify at the Java level without this sub-area.

classification_hints.summary: Pattern matching (instanceof, switch, record patterns, guard clauses), sealed classes, records, string templates, unnamed variables — Project Amber language features.
signals: Discusses pattern matching in switch/instanceof, record patterns, or sealed class hierarchies
References Project Amber or Amber-related JEPs
Covers records as data carriers or their pattern-matching integration
Discusses string templates (STR processor) or unnamed variables
classification_hints.boundary: If the article covers multiple Java language features broadly (e.g., 'What's new in JDK 25'), classify at the Java level. Only use this sub-area when pattern matching or Amber features are the central topic.

classification_hints.summary: Value classes, primitive types, null-restricted types, flat memory layout — Project Valhalla.
signals: Discusses value types, value classes, primitive classes, or null-restricted types
References Project Valhalla or Valhalla-related JEPs
Discusses flattened memory layouts, identity-free objects, or generic specialization
classification_hints.boundary: General JVM performance articles that don't specifically discuss value types or memory layout → Java level.

classification_hints.summary: Foreign Function & Memory API (FFM), Vector API, jextract — Project Panama for native interop.
signals: Discusses FFM API, MemorySegment, Arena, Linker, or SymbolLookup
References Project Panama or Panama JEPs
Discusses Vector API (jdk.incubator.vector) or SIMD on JVM
Covers jextract tool or JNI migration to FFM
classification_hints.boundary: Articles about legacy JNI usage (not FFM migration) → Java level.

classification_hints.summary: Ahead-of-time compilation, CDS archives, condensers, premain optimization — Project Leyden for JDK-native startup improvement.
signals: Discusses CDS (Class Data Sharing), AOT compilation within JDK, or Project Leyden
Covers startup time optimization at the JDK level (not GraalVM native image)
References Leyden condensers or premain image generation
classification_hints.boundary: GraalVM native image AOT → graalvm/native-image. General startup tips (class loading, lazy init) without Leyden specifics → Java level.

classification_hints.summary: Code reflection API, GPU compute on JVM, code models — Project Babylon.
signals: Discusses code reflection API, code models, or Project Babylon
Covers GPU/GPGPU programming from Java
References Babylon JEPs or HAT (Heterogeneous Accelerator Toolkit)
classification_hints.boundary: Standard reflection (java.lang.reflect, MethodHandles) → Java level.

classification_hints.summary: GC algorithms, tuning, and improvements — ZGC, Shenandoah, G1, Generational ZGC, Epsilon.
signals: Discusses specific GC algorithms (ZGC, Shenandoah, G1, Parallel, Serial, Epsilon)
Covers GC tuning parameters, pause time optimization, or throughput tuning
References Generational ZGC, concurrent marking, or GC barrier changes
Analyzes GC-related JEPs or GC benchmark results
Discusses Project Lilliput (compact object headers) for GC impact
classification_hints.boundary: General JVM performance articles that mention GC only briefly → Java level. GC-specific deep dives → this sub-area.

classification_hints.summary: JDK Enhancement Proposals — status changes, analysis, previews, and JDK release roadmap planning.
signals: Article is specifically about JEP proposals, status transitions (Draft → Candidate → Targeted → Integrated), or JDK feature planning
Analyzes upcoming JDK features across multiple JEPs (e.g., 'JDK 25 feature freeze analysis')
Discusses JEP process itself, OpenJDK governance, or release cadence
classification_hints.boundary: An article about a specific feature (virtual threads, pattern matching) that references a JEP number → classify under that feature's sub-area, not here. This sub-area is for JEP-centric or roadmap-centric content.

classification_hints.summary: JavaFX, OpenJFX, Swing — desktop GUI development on the JVM. Scene graph, FXML, CSS styling, desktop packaging.
signals: Discusses JavaFX, OpenJFX, Scene Builder, or FXML
Covers Swing modernization or desktop application packaging (jpackage, jlink)
References Java Verified Portfolio for desktop, or desktop-specific JDK features
classification_hints.boundary: Compose Multiplatform for desktop → kotlin/compose. General JDK features used on desktop → Java level.

classification_hints.summary: Reactive Streams, Project Reactor, RxJava, Mutiny — reactive programming patterns and libraries on the JVM.
signals: Discusses Project Reactor (Mono, Flux), RxJava, Mutiny, or Reactive Streams spec
Covers backpressure, reactive operators, or reactive vs virtual threads comparison
References R2DBC reactive database access or WebFlux reactive web
classification_hints.boundary: Virtual threads as reactive replacement → java/virtual-threads. Spring WebFlux specifics → spring. Quarkus Mutiny specifics → quarkus. This sub-area is for reactive programming as a paradigm.

classification_hints.summary: Articles about the Kotlin programming language, compiler, and Kotlin-specific ecosystem. Covers language features, toolchain, and Kotlin-first libraries.
topics: Kotlin language features and syntax (scope functions, delegation, DSL builders, context receivers)
Kotlin compiler (K2), KSP, compiler plugins
Kotlin ecosystem: Ktor, Exposed, kotlinx libraries
Kotlin community, KotlinConf, JetBrains announcements
not_this_node: Spring Boot applications written in Kotlin → spring (unless the article is about Kotlin-specific features)
Android-only Jetpack Compose → not covered by jvm-daily
Kotlin/JS or Kotlin/WASM in isolation (no JVM angle) → usually still kotlin

classification_hints.summary: Kotlin coroutines, Flow, channels, structured concurrency in Kotlin, kotlinx.coroutines library.
signals: Discusses Kotlin coroutines, suspend functions, Flow, StateFlow, SharedFlow
Covers channels, select expressions, or Kotlin-specific structured concurrency
Discusses coroutine scope, dispatchers, or cancellation patterns
classification_hints.boundary: General async/concurrency patterns that happen to use Kotlin → Kotlin level. Ktor's coroutine-based server → kotlin level or spring level depending on focus.

classification_hints.summary: Compose Multiplatform — declarative UI from Kotlin for desktop, web, and iOS.
signals: Discusses Compose Multiplatform (desktop, web, iOS targets — not Android-only)
Covers Compose state management, recomposition, or Composable functions in multiplatform context
classification_hints.boundary: Android-only Jetpack Compose → not covered by jvm-daily. Compose Multiplatform for multiple targets → this sub-area.

classification_hints.summary: Kotlin Multiplatform — sharing code across JVM, JS, Native, iOS, WASM. expect/actual, KMP library ecosystem.
signals: Discusses Kotlin Multiplatform project setup, expect/actual mechanism, or KMP libraries
Covers targeting multiple platforms from shared Kotlin code
References WASM target, Kotlin/Native, or KMP Wizard
classification_hints.boundary: Kotlin code that only targets JVM → Kotlin level. Compose Multiplatform UI → compose sub-area.

classification_hints.summary: K2 compiler, new Kotlin language features, KSP, compiler plugins, language design.
signals: Discusses K2 compiler, FIR, or compiler performance improvements
Covers new language features (context parameters, name-based destructuring, guard conditions)
References KSP (Kotlin Symbol Processing) or compiler plugin development
Discusses Kotlin language design decisions or KEEPs
classification_hints.boundary: Using existing Kotlin features in application code → Kotlin level. Compiler internals and new feature proposals → this sub-area.

classification_hints.summary: Articles about the Scala programming language and its ecosystem — Akka/Pekko, ZIO, Cats Effect, Play, Spark, sbt.
topics: Scala 3 language features (given/using, opaque types, enums, metaprogramming)
Scala ecosystem: Akka/Apache Pekko, ZIO, Cats Effect, http4s, Play Framework
sbt, Scala CLI, Metals, Scala tooling
Apache Spark (when Scala-focused, not pure data engineering)
not_this_node: Apache Spark purely as a data platform (no Scala code focus) → data-persistence
General functional programming concepts without Scala specifics → community

classification_hints.summary: Articles about the Spring framework family — Boot, Framework, Security, Cloud, Data, AI, WebFlux, Batch, Integration.
topics: Spring Boot auto-configuration, starters, actuator, DevTools
Spring Framework core (DI, AOP, WebMVC, WebFlux)
Spring Security (OAuth2, OIDC, CSRF, method security)
Spring Cloud (service discovery, config server, circuit breakers, gateway)
Spring AI (LLM integration, embeddings, vector stores, RAG)
Spring Data, Spring Batch, Spring Integration
not_this_node: Generic REST API design (not Spring-specific) → java
Quarkus or Micronaut → their own areas
Hibernate in isolation (not Spring Data JPA) → data-persistence
General security concepts (not Spring Security) → security

classification_hints.summary: Spring Boot — auto-configuration, starters, actuator, DevTools, embedded servers, Spring Initializr.
signals: Specifically about Spring Boot features: auto-configuration, starters, actuator, DevTools
Discusses Spring Boot release notes, migration guides, or Boot-specific configuration
Covers embedded Tomcat/Jetty/Netty in Spring Boot context
classification_hints.boundary: Spring Framework core (DI, AOP, WebMVC) without Boot specifics → Spring level. Spring Boot-specific behavior → this sub-area.

classification_hints.summary: Spring Security — OAuth2 resource server/client, OIDC, CSRF, session management, method security, SecurityFilterChain.
signals: Discusses Spring Security configuration, SecurityFilterChain, or authorization rules
Covers OAuth2/OIDC integration in Spring applications
References Spring Authorization Server or Spring Security release notes
classification_hints.boundary: General application security (CVEs, crypto, non-Spring auth) → security area.

classification_hints.summary: Spring Cloud — service discovery (Eureka, Consul), config server, circuit breakers (Resilience4j), API gateway, Stream, Function.
signals: Discusses Spring Cloud components or microservice patterns with Spring
Covers Spring Cloud Gateway, Spring Cloud Stream, or Spring Cloud Function
classification_hints.boundary: Generic microservice/distributed system patterns → infra area.

classification_hints.summary: Spring AI — LLM integration, chat models, embeddings, vector stores, RAG, function calling from Spring applications.
signals: Discusses Spring AI framework, AI model integration from Spring
Covers vector store integration (pgvector, Chroma, Milvus) via Spring AI
classification_hints.boundary: General LLM/AI articles not using Spring AI → not in this taxonomy. AI articles using Spring AI → this sub-area.

classification_hints.summary: Articles about Quarkus — supersonic subatomic Java framework. Dev Services, extensions, CDI, reactive with Quarkus, Quarkus CLI.
topics: Quarkus framework features, extensions, and Dev Services
Quarkus native compilation via GraalVM
Quarkus release notes, migration, and roadmap
RESTEasy Reactive, Mutiny, Qute templating in Quarkus
not_this_node: GraalVM native image in general (not Quarkus-specific) → graalvm
Jakarta EE specs without Quarkus context → java
MicroProfile without Quarkus context → java

classification_hints.summary: Articles about the Micronaut framework — compile-time DI and AOP, Micronaut Data, Micronaut Security, serverless support.
topics: Micronaut framework features and compile-time processing
Micronaut Launch, Micronaut Data, Micronaut Security
Micronaut release notes and migration guides
not_this_node: Generic compile-time annotation processing → java or build-tooling
Serverless deployment in general → infra

classification_hints.summary: Articles about Jakarta EE (formerly Java EE) specifications and implementations — JSF, CDI, JPA (spec level), JAX-RS, Servlet, EJB, MicroProfile.
topics: Jakarta EE specifications and releases (Jakarta EE 11+)
MicroProfile specifications and implementations
JSF (JavaServer Faces), PrimeFaces, OmniFaces
Application servers: WildFly, Payara, Open Liberty, GlassFish, TomEE
Migration from Java EE to Jakarta EE (javax → jakarta namespace)
not_this_node: Spring Framework (even though it implements some Jakarta specs) → spring
Quarkus (even though it supports CDI/JAX-RS) → quarkus
Hibernate/JPA as ORM technology → data-persistence/hibernate
Servlet containers (Tomcat, Jetty) used purely as embedded servers → infra

classification_hints.summary: Articles about GraalVM — native image compilation, Truffle language framework, polyglot runtime, GraalVM Community/Oracle editions.
topics: GraalVM native image compilation and optimization
GraalVM JIT compiler (Graal compiler) and performance
Truffle framework and polyglot interop (GraalJS, GraalPy, GraalWasm)
GraalVM releases, licensing, and roadmap
not_this_node: Quarkus native builds (Quarkus-specific) → quarkus
Micronaut AOT → micronaut
Project Leyden (JDK-native AOT) → java/leyden
Spring Native / Spring AOT → spring

classification_hints.summary: GraalVM Native Image — AOT compilation to standalone executables. Reflection configuration, build-time initialization, reachability metadata.
signals: Discusses native-image tool, ahead-of-time compilation to native binary
Covers reflection/resource/serialization configuration for native image
References GraalVM Reachability Metadata Repository or agent-based configuration
Discusses native image build time, image size, or startup time benchmarks
classification_hints.boundary: Truffle/polyglot without native image → GraalVM level. Framework-specific native image (Quarkus, Micronaut) → that framework's area.

classification_hints.summary: Truffle language implementation framework, polyglot interop, GraalJS, GraalPy, GraalWasm, Sulong.
signals: Discusses Truffle framework, language implementation on GraalVM, or polyglot runtime
Covers running JavaScript, Python, Ruby, R, or WASM on GraalVM
References GraalJS, GraalPy, or polyglot API
classification_hints.boundary: Just using GraalVM as a fast JVM (no polyglot) → GraalVM level.

classification_hints.summary: Articles about build systems, IDEs, developer productivity tools, and development experience for JVM projects.
topics: Gradle build scripts, plugins, build cache, configuration cache, performance
Maven POM configuration, plugins, dependency management, BOM
IntelliJ IDEA features, plugins, and developer productivity
Other tooling: jbang, sdkman, jenv, OpenRewrite
not_this_node: CI/CD pipeline configuration (Jenkins, GitHub Actions) → infra
Testing frameworks (JUnit, Testcontainers) → testing
IDE AI assistant features → not covered by this taxonomy

classification_hints.summary: Gradle build system — Kotlin DSL, Groovy DSL, plugins, build cache, configuration cache, version catalogs, dependency management.
signals: Specifically about Gradle build scripts, plugins, or Gradle daemon
Discusses build cache, configuration cache, or Gradle performance
Covers Gradle release notes or migration (Groovy → Kotlin DSL)
classification_hints.boundary: General build concepts (dependency resolution, reproducibility) → Build & Tooling level.

classification_hints.summary: Apache Maven — POM, plugins, lifecycle, dependency management, BOM, multi-module projects.
signals: Specifically about Maven POM configuration, plugins, or lifecycle
Discusses Maven release notes, Maven wrapper, or Maven daemon (mvnd)
classification_hints.boundary: General dependency management concepts → Build & Tooling level.

classification_hints.summary: IntelliJ IDEA, Eclipse, VS Code for Java/Kotlin — IDE features, plugins, code analysis, refactoring tools.
signals: Discusses IntelliJ IDEA features, Eclipse updates, or IDE plugins for JVM
Covers developer productivity tools: jbang, sdkman, OpenRewrite recipes
References IntelliJ release notes or IDE-specific announcements
classification_hints.boundary: Build tool features (Gradle, Maven) → their sub-areas. AI coding assistants → not in this taxonomy.

classification_hints.summary: Articles about testing frameworks, patterns, and practices for JVM projects.
topics: JUnit 5 features, extensions, parameterized tests, test lifecycle
Testcontainers for integration testing with Docker
Mocking: Mockito, MockK, WireMock
Testing patterns: TDD, BDD, mutation testing, property-based testing
ArchUnit, AssertJ, Awaitility, REST Assured
not_this_node: Performance benchmarking with JMH → java (performance is a JVM topic)
CI/CD test pipeline configuration → infra
Spring Boot testing (@SpringBootTest, MockMvc) → spring (unless the testing framework itself is the focus)

classification_hints.summary: JUnit 5 (Jupiter), JUnit Platform, TestNG — test framework features, extensions, migration from JUnit 4.
signals: Discusses JUnit 5 features, extensions, annotations, or test lifecycle
Covers TestNG features or JUnit 4 → 5 migration
References JUnit Platform, custom test engines, or parameterized tests
classification_hints.boundary: General testing advice without specific framework → Testing level.

classification_hints.summary: Testcontainers — disposable Docker containers for integration testing. Database, message broker, and service containers.
signals: Discusses Testcontainers library for Java or Kotlin
Covers using Docker containers in tests (database, Kafka, Redis containers)
References Testcontainers modules or Testcontainers Desktop
classification_hints.boundary: Docker in general (not for testing) → infra. Testcontainers-specific → this sub-area.

classification_hints.summary: Mockito, MockK, WireMock, mock servers — mocking and stubbing for unit and integration tests.
signals: Discusses Mockito, MockK, or mocking patterns in JVM tests
Covers WireMock or HTTP stubbing for API testing
References mock vs stub vs spy patterns
classification_hints.boundary: General test design (not mocking-specific) → Testing level.

classification_hints.summary: Articles about databases, ORM, data access layers, and persistence patterns on the JVM.
topics: Hibernate ORM, JPA specification, entity mapping, query optimization
JDBC, R2DBC, connection pooling (HikariCP), reactive data access
Database engines used with JVM: PostgreSQL, DuckDB, H2, CockroachDB
Database migration: Flyway, Liquibase
jOOQ, Exposed, JDBI, MyBatis
not_this_node: Spring Data JPA (Spring-specific) → spring (unless the Hibernate/JPA behavior itself is the focus)
Apache Kafka (event streaming, not persistence) → infra
In-memory caching (Redis, Caffeine) → infra

classification_hints.summary: Hibernate ORM, JPA specification — entity mapping, lazy loading, N+1 queries, HQL/JPQL, Criteria API, Hibernate 6+ features.
signals: Discusses Hibernate ORM features, JPA entity mapping, or query optimization
Covers Hibernate release notes, migration guides, or performance tuning
References HQL, JPQL, Criteria API, or Hibernate-specific annotations
classification_hints.boundary: Database design or SQL without ORM context → Data & Persistence level.

classification_hints.summary: JDBC, R2DBC, connection pooling (HikariCP), jOOQ, JDBI — low-level and mid-level data access.
signals: Discusses JDBC, R2DBC, connection pools, or raw SQL from JVM
Covers jOOQ type-safe SQL, JDBI, or MyBatis
References HikariCP, Agroal, or connection pool tuning
classification_hints.boundary: ORM-level concerns (entity mapping, lazy loading) → hibernate sub-area.

classification_hints.summary: Flyway, Liquibase — database schema migration tools and practices.
signals: Discusses Flyway or Liquibase migration scripts, versioning, or best practices
Covers database schema evolution strategies
classification_hints.boundary: General database design → Data & Persistence level.

classification_hints.summary: kotlinx.serialization, Jackson, Gson, Protocol Buffers, Avro, Parquet — serialization libraries and data format handling on JVM.
signals: Discusses JSON serialization (Jackson, Gson, Moshi, kotlinx.serialization)
Covers Protocol Buffers, Avro, Parquet, or other binary formats on JVM
References custom serializers, schema evolution, or format benchmarks
classification_hints.boundary: Database-specific serialization (JPA entity mapping) → hibernate sub-area. Generic data format handling → this sub-area.

classification_hints.summary: Articles about deploying, running, monitoring, and scaling JVM applications in production environments.
topics: Containers and Kubernetes for JVM apps (JVM in Docker, K8s resource limits, JVM ergonomics)
Serverless JVM (AWS Lambda, Google Cloud Run, Azure Functions, SnapStart)
Observability: Micrometer, OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, Grafana for JVM apps
Message brokers and event streaming: Kafka, RabbitMQ, Pulsar
Service mesh, API gateways, load balancing for JVM microservices
not_this_node: Build tooling (Gradle, Maven) → build-tooling
GraalVM native image compilation → graalvm
Spring Cloud components → spring/cloud
CI/CD tools (Jenkins, GitHub Actions) without JVM specifics → not in this taxonomy

classification_hints.summary: Docker, Kubernetes, Helm — containerizing and orchestrating JVM applications. JVM container awareness, cgroup limits, Jib, Buildpacks.
signals: Discusses Docker images for JVM apps, multi-stage builds, or distroless images
Covers Kubernetes deployment, resource limits, or JVM container ergonomics
References Jib, Cloud Native Buildpacks, or Paketo for JVM
classification_hints.boundary: Testcontainers (Docker for testing) → testing/testcontainers. General Docker/K8s (not JVM-specific) → only if JVM implications are discussed.

classification_hints.summary: AWS Lambda, Google Cloud Run, Azure Functions, AWS SnapStart — running JVM in serverless/FaaS contexts.
signals: Discusses JVM cold start optimization for serverless
Covers AWS SnapStart, CRaC (Coordinated Restore at Checkpoint), or similar
References Lambda layers, Cloud Run JVM configuration, or serverless framework for JVM
classification_hints.boundary: GraalVM native for serverless → graalvm/native-image. General serverless architecture (not JVM) → not in this taxonomy.

classification_hints.summary: Micrometer, OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, Grafana — metrics, tracing, and logging for JVM applications.
signals: Discusses Micrometer metrics, OpenTelemetry instrumentation, or distributed tracing in JVM apps
Covers Prometheus exporters, Grafana dashboards, or logging frameworks (SLF4J, Logback) for JVM
References observability standards or JVM-specific instrumentation
classification_hints.boundary: JVM-internal monitoring (JFR, JMX, jcmd) → java level. Application-level observability → this sub-area.

classification_hints.summary: Apache Kafka, RabbitMQ, Pulsar — message brokers and event streaming from JVM applications.
signals: Discusses Kafka clients, Kafka Streams, or consumer/producer patterns from JVM
Covers RabbitMQ, Pulsar, or AMQP integration in Java/Kotlin
References Spring Cloud Stream, Quarkus Messaging, or SmallRye Reactive Messaging
classification_hints.boundary: Spring Cloud Stream specifics → spring/cloud. Kafka as a platform (not JVM client) → only if JVM client code is discussed.

classification_hints.summary: Jenkins, GitHub Actions, TeamCity, GitLab CI — continuous integration and delivery pipelines for JVM projects. JVM-specific CI optimization.
signals: Discusses CI/CD pipeline configuration for JVM projects (Jenkins, GitHub Actions, TeamCity, GitLab CI)
Covers JVM-specific CI optimization (Gradle build cache in CI, test parallelization, incremental builds)
References deployment automation, release pipelines, or artifact publishing for JVM libraries
classification_hints.boundary: Build tool features (Gradle, Maven) → build-tooling. Container deployment → containers sub-area. CI/CD pipeline setup and optimization → this sub-area.

classification_hints.summary: Articles about JVM ecosystem security — CVEs, vulnerability patches, auth patterns, cryptography.
topics: CVE advisories affecting JDK, frameworks, or popular JVM libraries
Authentication and authorization patterns on JVM (OAuth2, OIDC, JWT, SAML)
Cryptography on JVM: JCA, Bouncy Castle, TLS configuration
Dependency vulnerability scanning: OWASP Dependency-Check, Snyk, Trivy
not_this_node: Spring Security (framework-specific) → spring/security
Quarkus Security → quarkus
General cybersecurity news without JVM relevance → not in this taxonomy

classification_hints.summary: Security vulnerabilities and patches in JDK, frameworks, and JVM libraries. CPU (Critical Patch Update) announcements.
signals: Discusses specific CVE numbers, security patches, or vulnerability advisories
Covers Oracle CPU or Red Hat security advisories for JDK
References dependency vulnerability scanning results
classification_hints.boundary: Security best practices (not specific CVE/patch) → Security level.

classification_hints.summary: OAuth2, OIDC, JWT, SAML, RBAC — auth patterns on JVM outside of specific framework integrations.
signals: Discusses auth patterns, JWT validation, OIDC flows, or token management on JVM
Covers Keycloak, Auth0, or identity provider integration (not Spring Security-specific)
classification_hints.boundary: Spring Security-specific auth configuration → spring/security.

classification_hints.summary: JCA, Bouncy Castle, TLS, encryption — cryptographic operations and secure communication on JVM.
signals: Discusses encryption, TLS configuration, certificate management, or JCA providers
Covers post-quantum cryptography on JVM or Bouncy Castle library usage
classification_hints.boundary: HTTPS configuration for a specific framework → that framework's area.

classification_hints.summary: Meta-articles about the JVM ecosystem — conferences, governance, developer surveys, ecosystem trends, notable people.
topics: JVM conferences: Devoxx, JavaOne, KotlinConf, SpringOne, QCon, Jfokus
Developer surveys and adoption data (JetBrains survey, Stack Overflow, New Relic JVM report)
OpenJDK governance, Java Champions, community leadership
Ecosystem-wide opinion pieces, trend analysis, language/framework comparisons
not_this_node: Technical conference talk content (about a specific technology) → classify by that technology
Conference speaker lineups or ticket sales → this area is fine (meta/community content)

classification_hints.summary: Articles about building AI-powered applications on the JVM — LLM integration, AI agents, embeddings, vector stores, inference, and AI-assisted development tools built in Java/Kotlin.
topics: LangChain4j, Semantic Kernel for Java, and other JVM AI frameworks
LLM inference on JVM (ONNX Runtime, DJL, llama.cpp JNI bindings)
AI agents and orchestration built in Java/Kotlin
Vector stores and RAG pipelines on JVM (outside Spring AI)
AI-assisted development tools for JVM: AI code review, AI testing, AI refactoring
Local LLM deployment and optimization for JVM applications
not_this_node: Spring AI specifically → spring/ai
General AI/ML news without JVM relevance → not in this taxonomy
AI coding assistants (Copilot, Cursor) as tools used BY developers → not in this taxonomy (these are tools, not JVM technology)

classification_hints.summary: LangChain4j, Semantic Kernel for Java, jlama — JVM-native AI/LLM frameworks (excluding Spring AI).
signals: Discusses LangChain4j, Semantic Kernel for Java, or similar JVM AI frameworks
Covers AI chain/pipeline construction, tool calling, or function calling from JVM
classification_hints.boundary: Spring AI → spring/ai. Generic LLM API calls without framework → AI & LLM on JVM level.

classification_hints.summary: Running AI models on JVM — ONNX Runtime, DJL (Deep Java Library), local LLM inference, model optimization.
signals: Discusses running ML/LLM models locally on JVM
Covers ONNX Runtime Java bindings, DJL, or llama.cpp JNI
References model quantization, inference optimization, or GPU acceleration from JVM
classification_hints.boundary: Using cloud LLM APIs (OpenAI, Gemini) from JVM → AI & LLM on JVM level. Running models locally → this sub-area.

classification_hints.summary: AI tools built for JVM development — AI code review, AI-powered testing, AI refactoring tools, AI documentation generators for Java/Kotlin codebases.
signals: Discusses AI code review tools specifically for JVM codebases
Covers AI-powered test generation, mutation testing with AI, or AI refactoring
References AI tools that analyze or improve Java/Kotlin code quality
classification_hints.boundary: General AI coding assistants (Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code) → not in this taxonomy. JVM-specific AI dev tools → this sub-area.

classification_hints.description: Article discusses migration requirements, API removals, deprecations, or backward-incompatible behavioral changes that require developer action.
signals: Mentions 'deprecated', 'removed', 'breaking change', 'migration required', or 'incompatible'
Discusses upgrade path, migration guide, or compatibility matrix
Warns about behavioral changes between versions

classification_hints.description: Article announces or explains a new feature, API, tool, or framework capability that didn't exist before.
signals: Introduces a new feature, API, tool, or library
Discusses preview/incubator features becoming stable
Announces new framework module or integration

classification_hints.description: Article focuses on speed, memory, startup time, throughput, or resource efficiency improvements.
signals: Presents benchmarks, performance comparisons, or optimization results
Discusses latency, throughput, memory footprint, or startup time
Covers GC tuning, JIT improvements, or runtime optimizations

classification_hints.description: Article reports a security vulnerability, CVE, patch, or security advisory requiring attention.
signals: Mentions CVE numbers, security patches, or vulnerability disclosures
Discusses Critical Patch Update (CPU) or security advisory
Recommends immediate upgrade due to security issue

classification_hints.description: Article discusses community-wide trends, governance changes, adoption shifts, or ecosystem evolution.
signals: Discusses ecosystem-wide trends, survey results, or adoption data
Covers governance decisions, licensing changes, or organizational shifts
